title: Recognizing the Warning Signs Behind the Wheel
summary: A specific, observable list of warning signs makes it easier to know when a driving conversation is actually overdue.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Recognizing the Warning Signs Behind the Wheel | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A specific, observable list of warning signs makes it easier to know when a driving conversation is actually overdue.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Slower reaction time, difficulty with signs or signals, and confusion at intersections are common early signs.
Family members riding along, even occasionally, is one of the best ways to observe these directly.
One incident isn't necessarily a pattern — but a cluster of them usually is.

paragraphs: Common warning signs include drifting between lanes, slower reaction to signals, difficulty judging distances or gaps in traffic, and increasing confusion at intersections or while merging. Individually, any one of these could be a bad day — together, they're a pattern worth acting on.

Riding along occasionally, even for short errands, gives family members a much clearer picture than secondhand reports or assumptions, and can also help build the case gently over time rather than all at once.

nextSteps: Ride along periodically to observe directly.
Keep a simple log if concerning incidents happen.
Bring specific, observed examples to the conversation rather than a general feeling.
disclaimer: General guidance, not a diagnostic checklist — a formal driving assessment can offer a more objective evaluation.

sources.name: Clinician's Guide to Assessing and Counseling Older Drivers
